channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Instagram's Threads gets 35 million new users this month
item.description: Nearly 35 million new users have flocked to Instagram's Threads this month as the Meta platform seeks to capture users leaving X and keep an edge over emerging platform Bluesky.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Arcane co-creator reveals the three 'amazing' League of Legends regions that'll be explored next, but there are two things he 'can't say' about them yet
item.description: Arcane's co-creator has finally confirmed which fan-favorite regions of Runeterra will be explored in the next League of Legends adaptation.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Elon Musk is sharing some details about his immigration path. Experts say they still have questions
item.description: By Catherine E. Shoichet | CNN It’s rare to hear Elon Musk discuss the details of his own immigration journey.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Revolutionizing HR and payroll: Strategies for a modern workforce
item.description: Learn how agencies are innovating their payroll services to meet evolving needs The post Revolutionizing HR and payroll: Strategies for a modern workforce first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Five key steps to transform UK government digital services
item.description: Lee Perkins analyses the 2022-2025 government digital roadmap, highlighting challenges and steps for successful transformation.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Escalar una empresa de dispositivos médicos: el recorrido de un CIO
item.description: Cuando comencé en Novanta hace alrededor de un lustro, mi primera misión fue brindar escalabilidad a nuestras soluciones empresariales, así como desarrollar una hoja de ruta digital para modernizar la huella tecnológica, reducir la deuda técnica y explorar estrategias para garantizar un crecimiento
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Automaker spying on drivers, reporting data to insurance companies
item.description: Since April, seven class action lawsuits have been filed against General Motors, OnStar, and data brokers LexisNexis and Verisk Analytics. The suits accuse GM of partnering with data brokers to collect driving data without proper consent using OnStar Smart Driver technology.
item.dc:creator: Mike Colombo, NewsNation

item.title: 3 data engineering trends riding Kafka, Flink, and Iceberg
item.description: Apache Kafka, Apache Flink, and Apache Iceberg are among the most popular technologies in the data ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Stanford prof accused of using AI to fake testimony in Minnesota case against conservative YouTuber
item.description: Jeff Hancock, an expert on AI and misinformation, has been accused of fabricating an expert declaration by citing a study that was allegedly made up by AI.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cheap Black Friday deals cost to your privacy
item.description: As Black Friday approaches, you should know that retailers often sell consumers' personal information and purchase histories to third parties.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bitcoin ticks closer to $100,000 in extended surge following US elections
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin extended its streak of record highs after ticking above $99,000 for the first time. The cryptocurrency has rocketed more than 40% in just two weeks.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TypeScript 5.7 arrives with improved error reporting
item.description: TypeScript 5.7, the latest planned release of Microsoft’s JavaScript variant with syntax for types, is now available in a production release. With version 5.7, the language’s type system is now able to report errors when variables have never been initialized.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: In the wake of Java Development Kit (JDK) 23, which arrived September 17, work now focuses on the planned successor release, JDK 24, which has ballooned to 24 proposed features. The four most recent additions include warnings upon the use of memory-access methods in sun.misc.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Supreme Court allows multibillion-dollar class action to proceed against Meta
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court is allowing a multibillion-dollar class action investors’ lawsuit to proceed against Facebook parent Meta, stemming from the privacy scandal involving the Cambridge Analytica political consulting firm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Pentagon rolls out new strategy for private 5G
item.description: DoD will rely on existing commercial networks "to the maximum extent possible," but in some cases, will need to run its own 5G infrastructure. The post Pentagon rolls out new strategy for private 5G first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: Backup Software vs. Backup as a Service (BaaS): The Key Differences
item.description: As data volumes increase exponentially, the threat of data loss from cyberattacks, human error, or system failure has never been greater — which is why, in 2025, fortifying a data protection strategy has never made more sense.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google faces scrutiny as DuckDuckGo calls for fresh EU probes into compliance
item.description: DuckDuckGo has urged the European Commission to open three investigations into Google’s compliance with the EU’s Digital Markets Act (DMA), alleging non-compliance with key obligations under the law.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nvidia is Wall Street's most valuable company. How it got there, by the numbers
item.description: Nvidia has once again turned out quarterly results that exceeded Wall Street's forecasts. The company has seen soaring demand for its semiconductors, which are used to power artificial intelligence applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Comcast to spin off cable networks that were once the entertainment giant's star performers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Comcast is spinning off into a new company many of the cable television networks that were once at the entertainment giant's heart as consumers increasingly swap out their cable TV subscriptions for streaming platforms.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US gathers allies to talk AI safety as Trump's vow to undo Biden's AI policy overshadows their work
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has vowed to repeal President Joe Biden's signature artificial intelligence policy when he returns to the White House for a second term. What that actually means for the future of AI technology remains to be seen.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How the U.S. Space Force can modernize at a smarter pace
item.description: Modernizing space programs while maintaining operations is one of the most challenging things, and rings true for the U.S. Space Force. The post How the U.S. Space Force can modernize at a smarter pace first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: Kotlin for Java developers: Classes and coroutines
item.description: Java is one of the classic object-oriented languages, so classes and objects are of special interest to Java developers. Object-oriented programming gives you considerable power, particularly when it comes to structuring and organizing programs as they grow larger.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft unveils imaging APIs for Windows Copilot Runtime
item.description: Microsoft’s Windows Copilot Runtime, which allows developers to integrate AI capabilities into Windows, is being fitted with AI-backed APIs for image processing. It will also gain access to Phi 3.5 Silica, a custom-built generative AI model for Copilot+ PCs.
item.dc:creator: Info World
